subject: Computer Networking
title: Greylisting Explained
description: Learn Greylisting Explained in one minute.

hook.text: Your server rejects the first delivery attempt. Legit servers retry. Spammers don't. That's greylisting.

buildup.text: On the first attempt, the server returns a 'try again later' code. It remembers the sender/recipient/IP combo.

discovery.text: Real mail servers retry after a few minutes. Spam bots move on to the next victim and never come back.

twist.text: Greylisting delays first-time emails by minutes. Users notice. Many admins disable it to avoid complaints.

climax.text: After the first retry succeeds, the pair is whitelisted. Future emails from that sender arrive instantly.

punchline.text: Greylisting rejects once, then allows forever. Simple and effective.

quiz.question: Why does greylisting work against spam bots?
options: Spam bots rarely retry after a temporary rejection
It encrypts the email so bots can't read it
It blocks all IPs from unknown countries
